017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.management;
018
019import java.io.*;
020import java.lang.management.*;
021import java.nio.file.*;
022
023import org.apache.juneau.rest.server.*;
024
025/**
026 * Shared worker for the diagnostic dump endpoints ({@code /threaddump}, {@code /heapdump}).
027 *
028 * <p>
029 * Both the {@code *Mixin} and {@code *Resource} flavors delegate here so the two forms cannot drift &mdash;
030 * the same shared-worker pattern the health and metrics surfaces use.
031 *
032 * <p>
033 * <b>Thread dump</b> is rendered from the JDK-standard {@link ThreadMXBean} (always available).
034 * <b>Heap dump</b> is produced via the HotSpot {@code HotSpotDiagnosticMXBean#dumpHeap(String,boolean)}
035 * method, invoked <b>reflectively</b> so this module carries no compile-time dependency on
036 * {@code com.sun.management} &mdash; on a non-HotSpot JVM the heap dump degrades cleanly (the endpoints
037 * surface that as HTTP 501).
038 *
039 * <p>
040 * Exposure is governed by {@link DumpsSettings} resolved from the bean store; both dumps are
041 * <b>deny-by-default</b> &mdash; see {@link DumpsSettings}.
042 *
043 * <h5 class='section'>See Also:</h5><ul>
044 *    <li class='jc'>{@link DumpsSettings}
045 *    <li class='link'><a class="doclink" href="https://juneau.apache.org/docs/topics/ManagementSurface">Management Surface</a>
046 * </ul>
047 *
048 * @since 10.0.0
049 */
050public class DumpsManager {
051
052   /**
053    * Resolves the {@link DumpsSettings} from the host context's bean store, falling back to the deny-all default.
054    *
055    * @param context The REST context whose bean store is searched.  May be <jk>null</jk>.
056    * @return The registered settings, or {@link DumpsSettings#DEFAULT} when none is registered.
057    */
058   @SuppressWarnings({
059      "resource" // The bean store is owned by the RestContext; this adapter only borrows beans and must not close it.
060   })
061   public DumpsSettings resolveSettings(RestContext context) {
062      if (context == null)
063         return DumpsSettings.DEFAULT;
064      return context.getBeanStore().getBean(DumpsSettings.class).orElse(DumpsSettings.DEFAULT);
065   }
066
067   /**
068    * Renders a full thread dump from the {@link ThreadMXBean}.
069    *
070    * @return A human-readable thread dump (never <jk>null</jk> or empty).
071    */
072   public String threadDump() {
073      var tmx = ManagementFactory.getThreadMXBean();
074      var infos = tmx.dumpAllThreads(true, true);
075      var sb = new StringBuilder();
076      for (var info : infos)
077         sb.append(info.toString());
078      return sb.toString();
079   }
080
081   /**
082    * Writes a heap dump (hprof) to the supplied file via the HotSpot {@code HotSpotDiagnosticMXBean}.
083    *
084    * <p>
085    * Invoked reflectively to avoid a compile-time dependency on {@code com.sun.management}.  The target file
086    * <b>must not already exist</b> (a {@code dumpHeap} requirement).
087    *
088    * @param target The hprof output file (must not pre-exist).
089    * @param live If <jk>true</jk>, dump only live (reachable) objects.
090    * @return <jk>true</jk> if the dump was written; <jk>false</jk> if this JVM has no HotSpot heap-dump support
091    *    (the endpoints surface that as HTTP 501).
092    */
093   public boolean heapDump(File target, boolean live) {
094      try {
095         var diagClass = Class.forName("com.sun.management.HotSpotDiagnosticMXBean");
096         @SuppressWarnings("unchecked")
097         var bean = ManagementFactory.getPlatformMXBean((Class<? extends PlatformManagedObject>) diagClass);
098         if (bean == null)
099            return false;  // HTT: only on a JVM exposing the class but no platform bean — not reproducible on HotSpot CI.
100         var m = diagClass.getMethod("dumpHeap", String.class, boolean.class);
101         m.invoke(bean, target.getAbsolutePath(), live);
102         return true;
103      } catch (ClassNotFoundException | NoSuchMethodException e) {
104         return false;  // HTT: non-HotSpot JVM with no heap-dump support — not reproducible on HotSpot CI (caller degrades to 501).
105      } catch (ReflectiveOperationException e) {
106         throw new RuntimeException("Failed to write heap dump to " + target.getAbsolutePath(), e);
107      }
108   }
109
110   /**
111    * Produces a heap dump and returns it as a self-deleting input stream.
112    *
113    * <p>
114    * Writes the hprof to a fresh temp file (the {@code dumpHeap} no-pre-existing-file requirement is satisfied
115    * by deleting the just-created temp file before the dump), then returns a stream over it that deletes the
116    * backing file when closed.
117    *
118    * @param live If <jk>true</jk>, dump only live (reachable) objects.
119    * @return A stream over the hprof file, or <jk>null</jk> if this JVM has no HotSpot heap-dump support.
120    * @throws IOException If the temp file could not be created or opened.
121    */
122   @SuppressWarnings({
123      "java:S5443" // Heap dump is an explicitly opt-in, admin-gated diagnostic; the random-named temp file is created then immediately reclaimed for dumpHeap.
124   })
125   public InputStream heapDumpStream(boolean live) throws IOException {
126      var f = File.createTempFile("juneau-heapdump-", ".hprof");
127      Files.delete(f.toPath());  // dumpHeap requires the target file to not already exist.
128      if (! heapDump(f, live)) {  // HTT: heapDump only returns false on a non-HotSpot JVM — not reproducible on HotSpot CI.
129         Files.deleteIfExists(f.toPath());
130         return null;
131      }
132      return new FileInputStream(f) {
133         @Override
134         public void close() throws IOException {
135            try {
136               super.close();
137            } finally {
138               Files.deleteIfExists(f.toPath());
139            }
140         }
141      };
142   }
143}
